name: Beagalltach Sword
description: Beagalltach, meaning 'Little Fury' or 'Little Fierce One,' is one of the two swords belonging to the Tuatha Dé Danann, a supernatural race in Irish mythology. It is the sister sword to Fragarach, 'The Answerer,' both forged by the mythical smith Goibniu. Beagalltach is often described as a sword that inflicts wounds from which no one recovers quickly, its power lying in its relentless and unforgiving nature. While Fragarach forces its wielder to tell the truth, Beagalltach ensures the truth is revealed through the pain it inflicts, making it a formidable weapon in battle. Its significance lies not only in its destructive capabilities but also in its connection to the divine craftsmanship of Goibniu and its relationship to its more famous counterpart, Fragarach. Together, the swords represent two sides of the same coin: truth and consequence, dominance and submission. Beagalltach is a symbol of power, pain, and the inevitable consequences of conflict, representing the harsh realities of the mythical world of the Tuatha Dé Danann. Though often overshadowed by Fragarach, Beagalltach's role as a weapon of relentless force is crucial to understanding the complexity of Irish mythology and the balance between power and responsibility. It's a grim reminder of the devastating impact of war and the lasting wounds it leaves behind. The sword is also seen as a symbol of justice, ensuring that wrongs are punished and that those who deserve retribution receive it. The sword's relentless nature makes it an instrument of divine wrath, a tool used to maintain order in a chaotic world. Its existence serves as a warning to those who would abuse their power or act unjustly, reminding them that their actions will have consequences.
subtitle: The Little Fierce One's Unforgiving Blade
material: Mythical Metal (likely enchanted iron or bronze)
origin: Forged by Goibniu, the smith of the Tuatha Dé Danann
currentLocation: Unknown, lost to time or hidden in the Otherworld

keyMyths.title: Forging of the Swords
keyMyths.description: Goibniu, the divine smith, forged Beagalltach and Fragarach, sister swords of immense power, for the Tuatha Dé Danann. Beagalltach's purpose was to inflict severe wounds, contrasting Fragarach's ability to extract truth.

keyMyths.title: Use in Battle
keyMyths.description: While specific instances of Beagalltach's use are less explicitly detailed than Fragarach's, it is understood to have been wielded by Tuatha Dé Danann warriors in various battles. Its presence ensured a swift and brutal end to conflict.
keyMyths.source: Indirectly referenced in battles of the Tuatha Dé Danann

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
sources.description: A collection and translation of early Irish myths and sagas, providing context for the Tuatha Dé Danann.
sources.text: A collection and translation of early Irish myths and sagas, providing context for the Tuatha Dé Danann.
